chunk_summary: The three-minute OPD audit evaluates Anganwadi supplementary nutrition delivery, focusing on registration, consumption, preparation barriers, and interruptions, with escalation protocols for corrective action.
learning_objectives: Understand the legal entitlement of supplementary nutrition under NFSA Schedule II standards.
Learn the steps of conducting a three-minute OPD audit for Anganwadi nutrition programs.
Identify barriers to effective nutrition delivery and consumption in pediatric populations.
Understand escalation protocols for addressing governance failures in Anganwadi services.
key_facts: Supplementary nutrition is a legal entitlement under NFSA Schedule II nutritional standards.
Audit steps include registration confirmation, receipt versus consumption analysis, and preparation barriers assessment.
Interruptions such as stock-outs, migration, or Anganwadi closures are considered governance failures.
Escalation protocol involves reporting through AWW, supervisor, sector in-charge, and CDPO.
Documentation includes anthropometry trends, feeding history, and a summary of nutrition receipt and consumption.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Supplementary nutrition through Anganwadi services is a legal entitlement anchored in the NFSA Schedule II nutrition standards, but state menus and delivery logistics vary.
